*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|12=draft}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=draft}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|12=draft}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|12=draft}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|12=draft}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=draft}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=draft}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|12=draft}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=draft}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=draft}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=draft}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=draft}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=draft}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=draft}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=draft}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|12=draft}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=draft}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|12=draft}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|12=draft}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|12=draft}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|12=draft}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|12=draft}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|12=draft}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|12=draft}}, etc.

; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|12=draft}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|12=draft}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|12=draft}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|12=draft}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|12=draft}} (in Nanyo)

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>

=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉}} (in Nanyo)
== Footnotes == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Spa Resort|ja|上山温泉郷}]] {{citation needed|かみのやまおんせん}}

revision.comment: 65 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:上山温泉 up to rev 103242794
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 上山温泉 |画像 = Meigetsu-so.JPG |コメント =外観 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |緯度度=38|緯度分=09|緯度秒=29.5 |経度度=140|経度分=16|経度秒=23.9 |所在地 = [[山形県]][[上山市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[奥羽本線]]・[[山形新幹線]] [[かみのやま温泉駅]]下車 |泉質 = [[塩化物泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク =[http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ かみのやま温泉] |特記事項 = }} '''上山温泉'''（かみのやまおんせん）は、[[山形県]][[上山市]]（旧国[[出羽国]]、[[明治]]以降は[[羽前国]]）にある[[温泉]]。
== 概要 == 山形県の温泉統計などの温泉地の名称は「上山（温泉）」であるが<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=山形県 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>、ひらがなの表記「'''かみのやま温泉'''」が用いられることも多い<ref name="siryo1" />。最寄りとなる[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）の駅名も「[[かみのやま温泉駅]]」である。
温泉街は湯町、新湯、十日町、河崎、高松、葉山、金瓶の7地区から構成されている。そのため、各地区をそれぞれ個々の温泉とみなし、'''上山温泉郷'''とも表現される。
山形県発行の統計情報「やまがたの温泉2024」において、湧出量は2,537ℓ／分と[[蔵王温泉]]に次ぐ県内2位、年度延利用者数は397,257人と県内4位、年度延宿泊利用人数は232,236人と県内2位となっている<ref name=":0" />。
古くは山形県[[鶴岡市]]の[[湯野浜温泉]]、[[福島県]]の[[東山温泉]]と共に「[[奥羽三楽郷]]」に数えられた<ref name="siryo1" />。
== 泉質 == * ナトリウム・カルシウム [[塩化物泉]]・[[硫酸塩泉|硫酸塩温泉]]（弱アルカリ性）<ref name="siryo1" />
== 温泉街 == 源泉数は5本（源泉温度は63〜69℃）で、[[1978年]]（昭和53年）から集中管理されている<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|date=|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />。
温泉街では多数の[[温泉宿|温泉旅館]]、[[温泉宿|温泉ホテル]]が営業している。また、[[共同浴場]]、[[日帰り入浴施設|日帰り温泉施設]]、[[足湯]]も存在する。[[2023年]]（令和5年）3月末現在、温泉利用施設数は32である<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |date= |accessdate=2024-02-15 |language=ja}}</ref>。
=== 旅館・ホテル ===
;* 高松葉山温泉 : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯]、[https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館]、[https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘]、[https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋]、[https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋]、[https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音]、寒河江屋旅館、[https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* 新湯温泉 : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル]、[https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋]、[https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館]、[https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋]、[https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館]、ホテル菊屋 ;* 湯町温泉 : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池]、ふぢ金旅館、ホテル山内、鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* 河崎温泉 : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館]
;* 十日町温泉 : しまづ旅館
=== 共同浴場・日帰り温泉施設 === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|下大湯共同浴場]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|二日町共同浴場]] 7軒の共同浴場が存在したが、一部は閉鎖されている。 ;* 下大湯（しもゆ）共同浴場<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室 |title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|date=|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されている<ref name="meikai" />。[[1624年]]開湯。 ;* 新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2024年2月末に閉鎖<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website=山形新聞|date=|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>。 ;* 二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されている<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2020年3月31日に閉鎖（休業）となり、[[温泉権]]をNPOに譲渡することが決定された<ref name="meikai" />。NPO法人（かみのやまランドバンク）が施設の運営を引き継ぐこととなり、建物を改修して2022年5月29日に営業を再開した。 ;* 湯町共同浴場<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2016年11月30日に閉鎖<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）<ref name="meikai" /> : 上山市老人福祉センター寿荘内にあり、上山市社会福祉協議会が運営している<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 中湯共同浴場<ref name="meikai" /> : 市営であったが2018年3月31日に閉鎖<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* その他 : 名人のゆ（湯町）、[https://kogenyu.com/ 高源ゆ]（蔵王坊平）
=== 足湯 === 5か所に足湯が整備されている（[[上山城]]、眉川橋、新湯、湯町、葉山）<ref name="siryo1" />。また、[[みゆき会病院]]の駐車場に私設の足湯が開放されている<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）である<ref name="siryo1" />。[[肥前国|肥前]]出身の旅の[[僧侶]]・月秀が、温泉で傷を癒している[[ツル|鶴]]を発見したとされる。そのため、古くは「鶴脛の湯」の別名があった。
[[江戸時代]]には当地に[[上山城]]および[[上山藩]]が置かれ、温泉地としてのみならず城下町としても繁栄した。また[[羽州街道]]の宿場町としても賑わいを見せた。
[[1878年]]（明治11年）7月には[[イザベラ・バード]]が来訪し、『[[日本奥地紀行]]』で外国人が容易に来られる場所であったら健康的な保養地となるであろうと紹介している<ref name="siryo1" />。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[奥羽本線]]・[[山形新幹線]]「[[かみのやま温泉駅]]」下車 * 自家用車 ** [[東北中央自動車道]]「[[山形上山インターチェンジ|山形上山IC]]」より[[国道13号]]経由 ** 東北中央自動車道「[[かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|かみのやま温泉IC]]」より国道13号等経由
== 周辺 ==
; 上山市内
* [[上山城]] * [[月岡公園 (上山市)|月岡公園]]（4月下旬[[サクラ|桜]]スポット） * [[武家屋敷 (上山市)|武家屋敷]] * [[斎藤茂吉記念館]] * [[蟹仙洞]] * 有形文化財（[[春雨庵]]、[[楢下宿]]） * [[リナワールド]] * [[蔵王カントリークラブ]] * 観光果樹園の[[果物]]狩り（[[サクランボ|さくらんぼ]]、[[ブドウ]]など） * [[蔵王連峰|蔵王]]（[[御釜 (蔵王連峰)|御釜]]、[[蔵王坊平ライザワールドスキー場|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場]]、[[蔵王猿倉|蔵王猿倉スキー場]]、[[蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ]]、[https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ 蔵王坊平高原ペンション村]、[https://zaopensionvillage.com/ 蔵王ペンション村]） * [[花咲山展望台]]（[[恋人の聖地]]） * [[上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ]] * [[丹野こんにゃく|丹野こんにゃく番所]] * [[上山競馬場#ニュートラックかみのやま|ニュートラックかみのやま]] * [[コストコ]]かみのやま倉庫店
; 上山市外周辺
* [[ぐっと山形]]、[[道の駅やまがた蔵王]]（お土産処・[[山形市]]） * [[黒沢温泉]]（山形市） * [[蔵王温泉]]（山形市） * [[山形蔵王温泉スキー場|蔵王温泉スキー場]]（山形市） * [[文翔館]]（山形市） * [[霞城公園]]（山形市） * [[立石寺]]（山寺・山形市） * [[上杉神社]]（[[米沢市]]） * [[上杉家廟所]]（米沢市） * [[熊野神社 (南陽市)|熊野神社]]（[[南陽市]]） * [[赤湯温泉 (山形県)|赤湯温泉]]（南陽市）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ 上山温泉郷]
{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かみのやまおんせん}} [[Category:山形県の温泉]] [[Category:上山市の地理]]

温泉街では多数の[[温泉宿|温泉旅館]]、[[温泉宿|温泉ホテル]]が営業している。また、[[共同浴場]]、[[日帰り入浴施設|日帰り温泉施設]]、[[足湯]]も存在する。[[2023年]]（令和5年）3月末現在、温泉利用施設数は32である<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |date= |accessdate=2024-02-15 |language=ja}}</ref>。
=== 旅館・ホテル ===
;* 高松葉山温泉 : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯]、[https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館]、[https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘]、[https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋]、[https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋]、[https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音]、寒河江屋旅館、[https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* 新湯温泉 : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル]、[https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋]、[https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館]、[https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋]、[https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館]、ホテル菊屋 ;* 湯町温泉 : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池]、ふぢ金旅館、ホテル山内、鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* 河崎温泉 : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館]
;* 十日町温泉 : しまづ旅館
=== 共同浴場・日帰り温泉施設 === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|下大湯共同浴場]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|二日町共同浴場]] 7軒の共同浴場が存在したが、一部は閉鎖されている。 ;* 下大湯（しもゆ）共同浴場<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室 |title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|date=|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されている<ref name="meikai" />。[[1624年]]開湯。 ;* 新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2024年2月末に閉鎖<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website=山形新聞|date=|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>。 ;* 二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されている<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2020年3月31日に閉鎖（休業）となり、[[温泉権]]をNPOに譲渡することが決定された<ref name="meikai" />。NPO法人（かみのやまランドバンク）が施設の運営を引き継ぐこととなり、建物を改修して2022年5月29日に営業を再開した。 ;* 湯町共同浴場<ref name="meikai" /> : 地元の温泉共同組合によって運営されていたが、2016年11月30日に閉鎖<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）<ref name="meikai" /> : 上山市老人福祉センター寿荘内にあり、上山市社会福祉協議会が運営している<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* 中湯共同浴場<ref name="meikai" /> : 市営であったが2018年3月31日に閉鎖<ref name="meikai" />。 ;* その他 : 名人のゆ（湯町）、[https://kogenyu.com/ 高源ゆ]（蔵王坊平）
=== 足湯 === 5か所に足湯が整備されている（[[上山城]]、眉川橋、新湯、湯町、葉山）<ref name="siryo1" />。また、[[みゆき会病院]]の駐車場に私設の足湯が開放されている<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 歴史 == 開湯は[[1458年]]（[[長禄]]2年）である<ref name="siryo1" />。[[肥前国|肥前]]出身の旅の[[僧侶]]・月秀が、温泉で傷を癒している[[ツル|鶴]]を発見したとされる。そのため、古くは「鶴脛の湯」の別名があった。
[[江戸時代]]には当地に[[上山城]]および[[上山藩]]が置かれ、温泉地としてのみならず城下町としても繁栄した。また[[羽州街道]]の宿場町としても賑わいを見せた。
[[1878年]]（明治11年）7月には[[イザベラ・バード]]が来訪し、『[[日本奥地紀行]]』で外国人が容易に来られる場所であったら健康的な保養地となるであろうと紹介している<ref name="siryo1" />。
== アクセス == * 鉄道 ** [[奥羽本線]]・[[山形新幹線]]「[[かみのやま温泉駅]]」下車 * 自家用車 ** [[東北中央自動車道]]「[[山形上山インターチェンジ|山形上山IC]]」より[[国道13号]]経由 ** 東北中央自動車道「[[かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|かみのやま温泉IC]]」より国道13号等経由
== 周辺 ==
; 上山市内
* [[上山城]] * [[月岡公園 (上山市)|月岡公園]]（4月下旬[[サクラ|桜]]スポット） * [[武家屋敷 (上山市)|武家屋敷]] * [[斎藤茂吉記念館]] * [[蟹仙洞]] * 有形文化財（[[春雨庵]]、[[楢下宿]]） * [[リナワールド]] * [[蔵王カントリークラブ]] * 観光果樹園の[[果物]]狩り（[[サクランボ|さくらんぼ]]、[[ブドウ]]など） * [[蔵王連峰|蔵王]]（[[御釜 (蔵王連峰)|御釜]]、[[蔵王坊平ライザワールドスキー場|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場]]、[[蔵王猿倉|蔵王猿倉スキー場]]、[[蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ]]、[https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ 蔵王坊平高原ペンション村]、[https://zaopensionvillage.com/ 蔵王ペンション村]） * [[花咲山展望台]]（[[恋人の聖地]]） * [[上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ]] * [[丹野こんにゃく|丹野こんにゃく番所]] * [[上山競馬場#ニュートラックかみのやま|ニュートラックかみのやま]] * [[コストコ]]かみのやま倉庫店

{{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かみのやまおんせん}} [[Category:山形県の温泉]] [[Category:上山市の地理]]

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉}} (in Nanyo)

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|12=draft|12=draft}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=draft|12=draft}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|12=draft|12=draft}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=draft|12=draft}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=draft|12=draft}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|12=draft|12=draft}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=draft|12=draft}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft|12=draft}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=draft|12=draft}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|12=draft|12=draft}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|12=draft|12=draft}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|12=draft|12=draft}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|12=draft|12=draft}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|12=draft|12=draft}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ|12=draft|12=draft}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|12=draft|12=draft}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|12=draft|12=draft}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|12=draft|12=draft}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ|12=draft|12=draft}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|12=draft|12=draft}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} (in Nanyo)

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).

In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Nanyo)

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Nanyo)
== Footnotes == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Spa Resort|ja|上山温泉郷}]] {{citation needed|かみのやまおんせん}}
