{{translated page|ja|上山温泉|version=103242794|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Salt springs]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kami-Yama]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }}
[[sn:Kaminoyama Onsen]] [[ja:上山温泉]] [[ja:山形県]] [[ja:羽田エアポートガーデン]] [[ja:東京都]] [[ja:熱海温泉]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:白浜温泉]] [[ja:和歌山県]] [[ja:小浜温泉]] [[ja:長崎県]] [[ja:瀬波温泉]] [[ja:新潟県]] [[ja:指宿温泉]] [[ja:鹿児島県]]
<!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen--><!--Kaminoyama Onsen-->

*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Nanyo)

revision.text:
*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama|qq=}} |Access = Railway: {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou Main Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shinkansen|qq=}}; alight at {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Station|qq=}} |Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chloride Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}} |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} in {{ill|Kami-Yama|ja|上山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama|qq=}} (formerly part of {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}}, and since the Meiji period, part of {{ill|Uzen Province|ja|羽前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uzen Province|qq=}}).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}, is also named {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Station|qq=}}.
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen|qq=}}), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsuruoka|ja|鶴岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruoka|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|qq=}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}, and {{ill|foot baths|ja|足湯|1=Ashiyu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/foot baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiyu|qq=}}. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|qq=}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|qq=}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|qq=}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Castle|qq=}}, Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}, discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|qq=}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Castle|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamiyama Domain|ja|上山藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Domain|qq=}}. It also prospered as a post town along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|ja|羽州街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushū Kaidō|qq=}}.
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|Isabella Bird|ja|イザベラ・バード|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isabella Bird|qq=}} visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the {{ill|Ou Main Line|ja|奥羽本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou Main Line|qq=}} and the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|ja|山形新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shinkansen|qq=}} – {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Station|ja|かみのやま温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Station|qq=}}. * By Private Car: ** From the {{ill|Tohoku Chūō Expressway|ja|東北中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Chūō Expressway|qq=}}: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|qq=}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 13|qq=}}, or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|qq=}} then along {{ill|National Route 13|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 13|qq=}}, etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * {{ill|Kamiyama Castle|ja|上山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kani-sen-dō|qq=}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harusame-an|qq=}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashita-juku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinaworld|qq=}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Country Club|qq=}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry|qq=}}, {{ill|Grapes|ja|ブドウ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grapes|qq=}}, etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao|qq=}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okama|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Sarukura|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|qq=}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|qq=}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanno Konnyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|qq=}} * {{ill|Costco|ja|コストコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Costco|qq=}} Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gutto Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|qq=}} (souvenir outlets in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山形市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata City|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Onsen|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen|ja|蔵王温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen Ski Resort|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōkan|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasumi Castle Park|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Tateishidera|ja|立石寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateishidera|qq=}} (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Shrine|qq=}} (in {{ill|Yonezawa|ja|米沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonezawa|qq=}}) * {{ill|Uesugi Family Mausoleum|ja|上杉家廟所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Family Mausoleum|qq=}} (Yonezawa) * {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|ja|熊野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Nanyo|ja|南陽市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanyo|qq=}}) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|qq=}} (in Nanyo)

revision.text:
*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|qq=}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|qq=}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|qq=}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|qq=}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|qq=}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kani-sen-dō|qq=}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harusame-an|qq=}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashita-juku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinaworld|qq=}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Country Club|qq=}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry|qq=}}, [[:en:Grape|Grapes]], etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao|qq=}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okama|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Sarukura|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|qq=}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|qq=}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanno Konnyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|qq=}} * [[:en:Costco|Costco]] Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gutto Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|qq=}} (souvenir outlets in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]]) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Onsen|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]] (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen Ski Resort|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōkan|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasumi Castle Park|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Yama-dera|Tateishidera]] (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Shrine|qq=}} (in [[:en:Yonezawa, Yamagata|Yonezawa]]) * [[:en:Yonezawa Uesugi clan cemetery|Uesugi Family Mausoleum]] (Yonezawa) * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)]] (in [[:en:Nan'yō, Yamagata|Nanyo]]) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|qq=}} (in Nanyo)
== Footnotes == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Spa Resort|ja|上山温泉郷}]]

*

The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”

In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|qq=}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|qq=}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|qq=}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|qq=}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|qq=}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kani-sen-dō|qq=}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harusame-an|qq=}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashita-juku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinaworld|qq=}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Country Club|qq=}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry|qq=}}, [[:en:Grape|Grapes]], etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao|qq=}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okama|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Sarukura|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|qq=}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|qq=}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanno Konnyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|qq=}} * [[:en:Costco|Costco]] Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gutto Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|qq=}} (souvenir outlets in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]]) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Onsen|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]] (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen Ski Resort|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōkan|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasumi Castle Park|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Yama-dera|Tateishidera]] (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Shrine|qq=}} (in [[:en:Yonezawa, Yamagata|Yonezawa]]) * [[:en:Yonezawa Uesugi clan cemetery|Uesugi Family Mausoleum]] (Yonezawa) * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)]] (in [[:en:Nan'yō, Yamagata|Nanyo]]) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|qq=}} (in Nanyo)

revision.text:
*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|qq=}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|qq=}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|qq=}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|qq=}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|qq=}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kani-sen-dō|qq=}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harusame-an|qq=}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashita-juku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinaworld|qq=}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Country Club|qq=}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry|qq=}}, [[:en:Grape|Grapes]], etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao|qq=}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okama|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Sarukura|qq=}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|qq=}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|qq=}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanno Konnyaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|qq=}} * [[:en:Costco|Costco]] Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gutto Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|qq=}} (souvenir outlets in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]]) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Onsen|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]] (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen Ski Resort|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōkan|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasumi Castle Park|qq=}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Yama-dera|Tateishidera]] (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Shrine|qq=}} (in [[:en:Yonezawa, Yamagata|Yonezawa]]) * [[:en:Yonezawa Uesugi clan cemetery|Uesugi Family Mausoleum]] (Yonezawa) * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)]] (in [[:en:Nan'yō, Yamagata|Nanyo]]) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|qq=}} (in Nanyo)

revision.text:
*
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|qq=}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|qq=}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|qq=}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|qq=}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|qq=}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|qq=}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|qq=}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{ill|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|qq=}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|qq=}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
