{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kaminoyama Onsen]][[ja:上山温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358865]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358865]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} *
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kaminoyama Onsen]][[ja:上山温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358865]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358865]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} *
{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
== Surroundings == ; Within Kami-Yama: * [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] * {{ill|Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|ja|月岡公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|1=Tsukioka Park (Kami-Yama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24868461}} (a cherry blossom spot in late April) * {{ill|Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|ja|武家屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai Residences (Kami-Yama)|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} * {{ill|Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|ja|斎藤茂吉記念館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|1=Saitō Mokichi Memorial Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500667}} * {{ill|Kani-sen-dō|ja|蟹仙洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kani-sen-dō|1=Kaisendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336739}} * Tangible Cultural Properties ({{ill|Harusame-an|ja|春雨庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harusame-an|1=Harusame-an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56010269}}, {{ill|Narashita-juku|ja|楢下宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narashita-juku|1=Narage-shuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541310}}) * {{ill|Rinaworld|ja|リナワールド|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinaworld|1=Lina World|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11348254}} * {{ill|Zao Country Club|ja|蔵王カントリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Country Club|1=Zao Country Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621928}} * Fruit picking at orchards ({{ill|Cherry|ja|さくらんぼ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cherry|1=Cherry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9072744}}, [[:en:Grape|Grapes]], etc.) * {{ill|Zao|ja|蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao|1=Zaou (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621925}} (including {{ill|Okama|ja|御釜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okama|1=Okama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489344}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja|蔵王坊平ライザスキー場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Liza World Ski Resort|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579621}}, {{ill|Zao Sarukura|ja|蔵王猿倉スキー場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Sarukura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵王猿倉|1=Zao Sarukura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621947}}, {{ill|Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|ja|蔵王坊平アスリートヴィレッジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Bōhei Athlete Village|1=Zaō Bōdaira Athlete Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621932}}, [https://www.zao-bodairapv.com/ Zao Bōhei High Plateau Pension Village], [https://zaopensionvillage.com/ Zao Pension Village]) * {{ill|Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|ja|花咲山展望台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|1=Hanasakiyama Observation Deck|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279903}} (the “Lovers’ Sanctuary”) * {{ill|Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|ja|上山市総合子どもセンターめんごりあ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Comprehensive Children's Center Mengoaria|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanno Konnyaku|ja|丹野こんにゃく番所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanno Konnyaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja|ニュートラックかみのやま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yama Racecourse New Track|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上山競馬場|1=Kaminoyama Racecourse|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358882}} * [[:en:Costco|Costco]] Kami-Yama Warehouse Store
; In the surrounding area: * {{ill|Gutto Yamagata|ja|ぐっと山形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gutto Yamagata|1=Gutto Yamagata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683547}}, {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|ja|道の駅やまがた蔵王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Yamagata Zao|1=Roadside station Yamagata Zao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123092259}} (souvenir outlets in [[:en:Yamagata (city)|Yamagata City]]) * {{ill|Kurosawa Onsen|ja|黒沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Onsen|1=Kurosawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678423}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]] (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja|蔵王温泉スキー場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Onsen Ski Resort|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山形蔵王温泉スキー場|1=Yamagata Zao Onsen Ski Resort|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8047625}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Bunshōkan|ja|文翔館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunshōkan|1=Bunshōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500081}} (Yamagata City) * {{ill|Kasumi Castle Park|ja|霞城公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasumi Castle Park|1=Kajō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660827}} (Yamagata City) * [[:en:Yama-dera|Tateishidera]] (a mountain temple in Yamagata City, also known as Yamadera) * {{ill|Uesugi Shrine|ja|上杉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (in [[:en:Yonezawa, Yamagata|Yonezawa]]) * [[:en:Yonezawa Uesugi clan cemetery|Uesugi Family Mausoleum]] (Yonezawa) * [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Nanyo)]] (in [[:en:Nan'yō, Yamagata|Nanyo]]) * {{ill|Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|ja|赤湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu Onsen (Yamagata)|1=Akayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635793}} (in Nanyo)

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Kamiyama Onsen |image = Meigetsu-so.JPG |Comment = Exterior view {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|38|09|29.5|N|140|16|23.9|E}}}} |Latitude=38|Latitude Minutes=09|Latitude Seconds=29.5 |Longitude=140|Longitude Minutes=16|Longitude Seconds=23.9 |Location = [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] |Access = Railway: [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]]; alight at [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]] |Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] |Spring Temperature = |Yield = |pH = |Fluid Classification = |Osmotic Classification = |Number of Lodging Facilities = |Total Capacity = |Annual Number of Bathers = |Statistical Year = |External Links = [http://kaminoyama-spa.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kamiyama Onsen|ja|かみのやま温泉}]] |Notes = }} '''Kamiyama Onsen''' (かみのやまおんせん) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] in [[:en:Kaminoyama|Kami-Yama]] (formerly part of [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and since the Meiji period, part of [[:en:Uzen Province|Uzen Province]]).
== Overview == Although official statistics published by Yamagata Prefecture refer to the hot spring area simply as “Kamiyama (Onsen)”<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=山形県の温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/050011/kenfuku/doubutsuaigo/eisei/seikatsueisei/onsen/onsen_yamagata/index.html |website=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja |last=山形県}}</ref>, the hiragana representation “'''かみのやま温泉'''” is also frequently used.<ref name="siryo1" /> The nearest station, operated by [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]], is also named [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]].
The hot spring town is composed of seven areas—Yumachi, Shinyu, Tokkamachi, Kawazaki, Takamatsu, Hayama, and Kanpei—so that each area is regarded as its own hot spring; the entire region is sometimes called “Kamiyama Onsen-gō.”
According to the statistical information “やまがたの温泉2024” published by Yamagata Prefecture, the discharge rate is 2,537 ℓ/min (Ranking second in the prefecture after [[:en:Zaō Onsen|Zao Onsen]]), the annual number of users is 397,257 (Ranking fourth), and the annual number of overnight users is 232,236 (Ranking second).<ref name=":0" />
In earlier times, it was counted together with {{ill|Yunohama Onsen|ja|湯野浜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohama Onsen|1=Yunohama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563805}} in [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]], Yamagata Prefecture, and {{ill|Higashiyama Onsen|ja|東山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Onsen|1=Higashiyama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526730}} in Fukushima Prefecture as part of the “{{ill|Ōu Sanrakugō|ja|奥羽三楽郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōu Sanrakugō|qq=}}”.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Spring Quality == * The spring water is a sodium–calcium chloride spring combined with a sulfate spring (weakly alkaline).<ref name="siryo1" />
== Hot Spring Town == There are five sources (with source temperatures ranging from 63–69℃) that have been centrally managed since 1978 (Shōwa 53).<ref name="siryo1">{{Cite web|和書|author=山形県上山市 クアオルト推進室|title=かみのやま温泉クアオルトスタイル|url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/council/yuushikisya/02yuushikisya/siryo1.pdf|website=環境省|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
The town features many {{ill|Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562938}} and {{ill|Onsen Hotel|ja|温泉ホテル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579612}} establishments. In addition, there are communal baths, {{ill|day-use onsen facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use onsen facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ashiyu|Ashiyu]]. As of the end of March 2023 (Reiwa 5), the total number of onsen facilities is 32.<ref name="yamagata2023">{{Cite web|和書 |title=やまがたの温泉 |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/2339/yamagatanoonsen2023.pdf |website=山形県環境エネルギー部みどり自然課 |accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref>
=== Ryokan and Hotels === ;* {{ill|Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja|高松葉山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Hayama Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579613}} : [https://www.koyoga.com/ 日本の宿古窯], [https://www.hayamakan.com/ 葉山館], [https://www.meigetsuso.co.jp/index.html 名月荘], [https://www.hasimotoya.com/ はたごの心橋本屋], [https://saikatei-jidaiya.jp/ 彩花亭時代屋], [https://mori-ne.com/ おやど森の音], 寒河江屋旅館, [https://sansorai.com/ 三木屋参蒼来] ;* {{ill|Shinyu Onsen|ja|新湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塩原新湯温泉|1=Shiobara-Arayu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428629}} : [https://www.tsukioka.co.jp/ 仙渓園月岡ホテル], [https://azuma-ya.co.jp/ 果実の山あづま屋], [https://www.arimakan.com/index.php 有馬館], [https://zaimokusakaeya.ytbldg.jp/ ステイインホテル材木栄屋], [https://onsen-hataya.com/ はたや旅館], ホテル菊屋 ;* {{ill|Yumachi Onsen|ja|湯町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://www.tsukinoike.co.jp/ 花明かりの宿月の池], ふぢ金旅館, ホテル山内, 鶴の湯長十郎旅館 ;* {{ill|Kawazaki Onsen|ja|河崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazaki Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : [https://hpdsp.jp/fujiyaryokan/ ふじや旅館] ;* {{ill|Tokkamachi Onsen|ja|十日町温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokkamachi Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : しまづ旅館
=== Communal Baths and Day-Use Facilities === [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Shimo-Oyu.jpg|thumb|right|Shimooyu Communal Bath]] [[File:Kaminoyama Onsen Futsukamachi.jpg|thumb|right|Futsukamachi Communal Bath]] There are 7 communal baths, though some have closed. ;* {{ill|Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja|下大湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimooyu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579614}}<ref name="meikai">{{Cite web|和書|author=明海大学 不動産学部 小杉学研究室|title=かみのやまランドバンクエリアの取り組み 2018-2022|url=https://meikai-re.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/220928kaminoyama-matome.pdf|website=明海大学|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. Opened in 1624. ;* {{ill|Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja|新丁温泉共同浴場（鶴の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchō Onsen Communal Bath (Tsurunoyu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579615}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed at the end of February 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=今月限り、惜しむ声多く 上山・新丁温泉共同浴場「鶴の湯」|url=https://www.yamagata-np.jp/news/202402/15/kj_2024021500371.php|website={{ill|Yamagata Shimbun|ja|山形新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Shimbun|1=Yamagata Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468452}}|accessdate=2024-02-15|language=ja}}</ref> ;* {{ill|Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja|二日町共同浴場（親子集いの湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsukamachi Communal Bath (Oyako Tsudoi no Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579616}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative. ;* {{ill|Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja|新湯共同浴場（通称澤の湯）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinyu Communal Bath (Sawano Yu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579617}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative; it was closed on March 31, 2020 (suspended) and the onsen rights were transferred to an NPO. The NPO (Kamiyama Land Bank) took over and reopened the facility on May 29, 2022 after renovations. ;* {{ill|Yumachi Communal Bath|ja|湯町共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumachi Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579619}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Operated by the local onsen cooperative, but closed on November 30, 2016. ;* {{ill|Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja|葉山公衆浴場（寿荘）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Public Bath (Jusō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579620}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Located within the Kami-Yama Elderly Welfare Center Jusō and operated by the Kami-Yama Social Welfare Council. ;* {{ill|Nakayu Communal Bath|ja|中湯共同浴場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayu Communal Bath|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491604}}<ref name="meikai" /> : Previously municipally operated, but closed on March 31, 2018. ;* Others: : “Myōjin no Yu” (in Yumachi) and [https://kogenyu.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kogen-yu|ja|高源ゆ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogen-yu|qq=}}] (at Zao Bōhei)
=== Foot Baths === There are 5 foot baths located at [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]], Mayugawa Bridge, Shinyu, Yumachi, and Hayama.<ref name="siryo1" /> Additionally, a private foot bath is available in the parking lot of {{ill|Miyuki-kai Hospital|ja|みゆき会病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki-kai Hospital|1=Miyukikai Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30933064}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=足湯「みゆきの湯」 |url=https://www.miyuki.or.jp/hp/facilities-hp/315 |website=社会医療法人みゆき会 |access-date=2025-01-05 |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == The hot spring was first opened in [[:en:1458|1458]] (Chōroku 2).<ref name="siryo1" /> It is said that Tsukihide, a traveling [[:en:Monk|Monk]] from [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], discovered a {{ill|crane|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} bathing in the spring to heal his wounds. For this reason, it was formerly also known as “Tsuruhiza no Yu” (Crane’s Leg Spring).
During the Edo period, Kamiyama Onsen flourished not only as a hot spring resort but also as a castle town with [[:en:Kaminoyama Castle|Kamiyama Castle]] and the [[:en:Kaminoyama Domain|Kamiyama Domain]]. It also prospered as a post town along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]].
In July 1878 (Meiji 11), [[:en:Isabella Bird|Isabella Bird]] visited and, in her book [[Nihon Oku Chikukō]], described the area as a potentially healthy resort should it become easily accessible to foreigners.<ref name="siryo1" />
== Access == * Railway: ** Alight at the station on the [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ou Main Line]] and the [[:en:Yamagata Shinkansen|Yamagata Shinkansen]] – [[:en:Kaminoyama-Onsen Station|Kamiyama Onsen Station]]. * By Private Car: ** From the [[:en:Tōhoku-Chūō Expressway|Tohoku Chūō Expressway]]: either via the {{ill|Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|ja|山形上山インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Kamiyama Interchange|1=Yamagata-Kaminoyama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11045373}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], or via the Tohoku Chūō Expressway {{ill|Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|ja|かみのやま温泉インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Onsen Interchange|1=Kaminoyama-Onsen interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867377}} then along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13]], etc.
